seo_title: Meta Employees Protest Mandatory Laptop Surveillance for AI Training
meta_description: Meta employees are organizing against corporate software that tracks workers' keystrokes and mouse activity, raising concerns about employee privacy and data…
body_html:
Meta employees are organizing against corporate software that tracks workers' keystrokes and mouse activity, with the goal of collecting data for AI training.

Key Concerns About Employee Data Collection

Employees have expressed concerns about non-consensual data extraction for AI training purposes. The petition demands an end to what Meta calls the Model Capability Initiative, a piece of mandatory software that records employees' screens when using certain apps. Meta has yet to say whether the initial data is paying off, raising questions about its effectiveness and impact on employee privacy.

Meta Mandatory software tracks employees' keystrokes and mouse activity for AI training.

Other companies Typically tap volunteers, sometimes paid, to gather data for agentic AI models.

Regulatory Landscape and Safety Concerns

In the US, employers generally have wide latitude to monitor workers' devices for security, training, evaluation, and safety purposes. However, using these tools to build datasets that instruct AI systems on navigating computers without human supervision appears to be a new tactic that doesn't sit right with many Meta workers.

source.title: An Engineer’s Post Protesting Laptop Surveillance Is Going Viral Inside Meta
source.summary: Meta employees in the US and UK are organizing against corporate software that tracks workers’ keystrokes and mouse activity.
